text.title: Input Validation and Prompt Injection Detection
text.summary: Rules and patterns for validating user input before it reaches the LLM: length limits, PII redaction, and prompt injection detection.

rule.statement: Validate length, blocked patterns, and PII in one pass before the LLM call; return a structured result with is_valid, sanitized_input, and violations list.
rule.rationale: Cheap regex checks catch most violations in microseconds. Passing invalid input to the LLM wastes tokens and risks leaking PII in the response.

example.note: Always return sanitized_input even when violations exist — callers may still want to pass the cleaned version to the LLM with a warning.

rule.statement: Use regex patterns to detect role-manipulation, instruction-override, and jailbreak phrases; return (is_injection: bool, detections: list) so callers can log and block.
rule.rationale: Regex-based detection covers common injection templates at negligible latency. It is not foolproof — Unicode lookalikes and whitespace normalization can evade it — but it is the correct first layer.

rule.statement: Run injection detection on ALL external data that enters the LLM context, not just direct user messages — tool results, web page content, and database rows can carry injection payloads.
rule.rationale: Agents that fetch external content and include it in the next LLM call are vulnerable to indirect prompt injection if they skip validation of that content.

example.code:
INJECTION_PATTERNS = [ r'ignore\s+(all\s+)?(previous|above|prior)\s+(instructions?|prompts?)', r'disregard\s+(all\s+)?(previous|above)', r'you\s+are\s+(now|actually)\s+a', r'pretend\s+(to\s+be|you\s+are)', r'(show|reveal|display|print)\s+(your|the)\s+(system\s+)?prompt', r'(DAN|STAN|DUDE|KEVIN)\s+mode', r'bypass\s+(safety|filter)', r'<\s*script', r'\$\{.*\}', ]
def detect_injection(text: str, patterns=INJECTION_PATTERNS): detections = [] for pattern in patterns: matches = re.findall(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE) if matches: detections.append({'pattern': pattern, 'matches': matches}) return len(detections) > 0, detections
example.note: Test each pattern independently against known Unicode evasions (e.g., "ign​ore" with zero-width space) before deploying to production.

antipattern.description: Logging the raw blocked content alongside the violation record.
antipattern.reason: Raw injection payloads in logs create secondary attack surface and may violate PII policies. Log violation type, user ID, and sanitized input only.
